what: Verify the user has valid Anthropic access (CLI subscription or API key)
why: Nori uses Claude CLI or Anthropic SDK for all AI operations — access must be validated before the app is usable

success_handling.criteria: claude auth status returns loggedIn:true, OR ANTHROPIC_API_KEY validates against GET /v1/models

error_handling.scenario: Not logged in and no API key
error_handling.criteria: claude auth status returns loggedIn:false AND ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set
error_handling.action: Set has_anthropic_access=false, access_type=none. SettingsPage shows instructions.
Fall through to none. SettingsPage shows invalid key warning.

error_handling.scenario: Network unreachable during API key validation
error_handling.action: validateApiKey returns false. Flow continues; has_anthropic_access=false.

decisions.reason: CLI auth checked first, API key second
decisions.rationale: CLI auth provides richer metadata (email, subscription_type). API key is the fallback for programmatic users without a Claude account.

decisions.reason: GET /v1/models used for API key validation, not a messages call
decisions.rationale: Models endpoint is free — no tokens consumed, no side effects. Returns 200 for valid keys, 401 for invalid.

decisions.reason: Both --output-format json and plain text fallback for claude auth status
decisions.rationale: The --output-format flag may not exist in older CLI versions. JSON parse attempted first; text heuristics used as fallback.
